content.full_text:
MEMO TO THE FILE

FROM:  MJB

RE:    TELEPHONE CONVERSATION WITH THOMAS LOGIE 3/10/76

       Paul Schaffer asked me to call Thomas Logie of the

Pacific Lighting Corporation (213/689-3267) respecting

the I.G.A. decision. I advised Mr. Logie that the Commission

was restrained by a preliminary injunction from enforcing the

term "arrange" in section 86202 to mean that a lobbyist was

prohibited from simply recommending or advising his employer

that a political contribution should be made. I told Mr. L.

that the injunction extended only to the FPPC, and that,

theoretically, the Attorney General could institute a

criminal action under the section or a private individual

could initiate a civil injunctive action under §91003.

I told him that, as a practical matter, I considered it

extremely unlikely that the A.G. would institute a proceeding.

Moreover, if he did or if an individual instituted an action,

I thought it extremely unlikely that it would get very far

in view of the pending IGA appeal.


MJB:pvp

sections.question_synthetic: This document does not contain a formal legal question; it is an internal FPPC memorandum documenting a telephone conversation regarding the I.G.A. decision and the enforceability of the term 'arrange' in section 86202.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an internal memorandum, not a formal advice letter response. No Q/C sections present. Document records staff guidance on preliminary injunction implications regarding lobbying activities and political contributions. Extracted information limited to available content structure.

I told Mr. L. that the injunction extended only to the FPPC, and that, theoretically, the Attorney General could institute a criminal action under the section or a private individual could initiate a civil injunctive action under §91003. embedding.summary: Internal FPPC memorandum documenting a March 10, 1976 telephone conversation between FPPC staff and Thomas Logie of Pacific Lighting Corporation regarding a preliminary injunction restraining the Commission from enforcing certain provisions of section 86202 related to lobbyist activities and political contributions.
